facts.text: Boston was founded in 1630 on the Shawmut Peninsula, and its settlers named it after their hometown of Boston, Lincolnshire, England.
In 1852, Boston Public Library opened as the first free library in the United States.
Boston Latin School, founded in 1635, is America's oldest public school in the nation.
Back Bay was created by filling the former hills with earth and gravel, and today it hosts landmarks like the Boston Public Library and Trinity Church.
The Boston Celtics, founding members of the NBA, have won eighteen championships, the most of any team.
Puritan ethics shaped Boston even before its founding, with the 1629 Cambridge Agreement establishing the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
